description: This API routine is obsolete. It has been replaced by the API routine scal_4, and is implemented by a call to scal_4. Use this API routine to uniformly increase or decrease any or all specified bus quantities for a specified group of buses. It must be referenced using one of the following procedures: - One reference with APIOPT=1, followed by one reference with APIOPT=2. - One reference with APIOPT=0. This automatically combines the processing of one APIOPT 1 reference, followed by one APIOPT 2 reference. This API routine was first introduced in release 33.1.0. ierr, totals, moto= scal_2(sid, all, apiopt, status, scalval)
python_syntax: ierr, totals, moto= scal_2(sid, all, apiopt, status, scalval)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem identifier. valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. subsystem sid must have been previously defined. (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag . (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: apiopt
parameters.description: Mode of operation in the api . (input; 0 by default).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: An array of 5 elements specifying control scaling. The first and fifth elements are checked and saved when APIOPT = 0 or 1; the second, third and fourth elements are checked and used when APIOPT = 0 or 2. (input).

parameters.name: scalval
parameters.description: An array of 7 elements specifying scaling targets. Based on the value of STATUS(1), entries (1) through (6) are either total powers (present total power by default), percent changes (0.0 by default), or incremental changes (0.0 by default). If STATUS(4) = 2, SCALVAL(7) = new total Mvar load (present total Mvar load by default). If STATUS(4) = 3, SCALVAL(7) = percent change (0.0 by default). If STATUS(4) = 4, SCALVAL(7) = new power factor (1.0 by default). If STATUS(4) = 5, SCALVAL(7) = incremental Mvar load change (0.0 by default). (input).

return_values.name: totals
return_values.description: An array of 11 elements specifying returned when APIOPT = 0 or 1. (output).

return_values.name: moto
return_values.description: Returned when apiopt = 0 or 1. it indicates the presence of motors that are modeled as conventional generators with negative active power settings. (output).

all.description: process only buses in subsystem SID.

apiopt.description: initialize for scaling, then run the scaling and post-processing housekeeping.
run the scaling and post-processing housekeeping.

status.description: interruptible load scaling option (0 by default).
scale both interruptible and non-interruptible scalable loads.
method used to scale active power load, generation and motor data, and bus shunt data (0 by default).
flag to enforce machine power limits (0 by default).
flag to specify the scaling rule to be enforced when changing the reactive power load (0 by default).
is the bus type code flag for load scaling; normally defaulted except when used by OPF (0 by default).
only Type 2 and 3 buses in subsystem.

moto.description: no motors in the specified subsystem.
motors present in the specified subsystem.
